16.reason: // The problem states vertices are labeled 0 to n-1, but here n is decremented, causing incorrect graph size handling.

reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-sc-1: 0.25 , reason:  [Incorrectly decrements n, which changes the actual number of vertices. The problem states vertices are labeled 0 to n-1, so n should remain as input. This causes array bounds and traversal logic to be off by one.]
reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-sc-2: 0.3333333333333333 , reason:  [The problem states vertices are labeled 0 to n-1 for n vertices, but this decrements n before building the graph, causing all subsequent logic to treat the graph as having n-1 vertices instead of the actual n. This leads to incorrect adjacency matrix bounds and traversal ranges.]
reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-sc-3: 0.25 , reason:  [Incorrectly decrements n, which causes the graph to be treated as having one fewer vertex than actually provided. This leads to incorrect traversal bounds and missing the last vertex.]
reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-sc-4: 0.5 , reason:  // The problem states vertices are labeled 0 to n-1, but here n is decremented, causing incorrect graph size handling.
reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-sc-5: 0.3333333333333333 , reason:  [The problem states vertices are labeled 0 to n-1, but here n is decremented immediately after reading, causing all subsequent logic to treat the graph as having n-1 vertices instead of n. This leads to incorrect traversal bounds and missing the last vertex.]

60.reason: [Because 'n' was decremented in main(), this loop runs from 0 to n-1 (original n-1), but should run from 0 to original n - 1. However, since n was already reduced, this actually excludes the highest-numbered vertex (original n-1) from being considered during traversal, leading to incomplete DFS.]

reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-sc-1: 0.25 , reason:  [Loop condition uses <= n, but since n was decremented in main, this may access out-of-bound indices or miss the last vertex. Should be p < original_n, but original_n is lost due to n--.]
reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-sc-2: 0.3333333333333333 , reason:  [Because 'n' was decremented in main(), this loop runs from 0 to n-1 (original n-1), but should run from 0 to original n - 1. However, since n was already reduced, this actually excludes the highest-numbered vertex (original n-1) from being considered during traversal, leading to incomplete DFS.]
reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-sc-3: 0.25 , reason:  [Loop condition uses <= n, but since n was decremented in main, this excludes the actual last vertex (original n-1). Should loop up to original n-1, not modified n.]
reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-sc-5: 0.3333333333333333 , reason:  [Because 'n' was decremented in main(), this loop runs from 0 to n-1 (original n-2), so it never checks adjacency for the original last vertex (n-1). This causes incomplete DFS traversal.]

78.reason: [BFS queue logic uses 'head <= tail' which may cause reading beyond written elements. Standard BFS uses 'head < tail'. Also, 'tail' is used as next insertion index, so condition should be head < tail.]

reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-sc-1: 0.25 , reason:  [BFS queue logic uses 'head <= tail' which may cause reading beyond written elements. Standard BFS uses 'head < tail'. Also, 'tail' is used as next insertion index, so condition should be head < tail.]
reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-sc-3: 0.25 , reason:  [Condition should be head < tail for standard BFS queue handling. Using <= may access uninitialized elements beyond the current queue tail.]

90.reason: // C89 standard does not allow variable declaration inside for loop; this causes compilation error in strict C compilers.

reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-sc-1: 0.25 , reason:  [Same issue: loop bound uses decremented n, leading to incorrect traversal range. Should iterate up to original n (exclusive), but n was reduced by 1.]
reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-sc-2: 0.3333333333333333 , reason:  [Same issue as in len(): due to n being decremented, this BFS loop does not consider adjacency to vertex with original label n-1, causing incorrect traversal after deletion or even before if that vertex is connected.]
reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-sc-3: 0.25 , reason:  [Same issue as in len(): loop bound is wrong due to earlier n--. Also, variable-length array or fixed size may be okay, but logic fails due to incorrect n.]
reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-sc-4: 0.5 , reason:  // C89 standard does not allow variable declaration inside for loop; this causes compilation error in strict C compilers.
reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-sc-5: 0.3333333333333333 , reason:  [Same issue: loop bound uses decremented 'n', so BFS also misses the original last vertex (n-1), leading to incomplete traversal.]
